parts.prompt: On the axes provided, sketch a slope field for the given differential equation at the twelve points indicated. (Note: Use the axes provided in the pink test booklet.)

scoring_points.description: Correct zero slopes on slope field

required_elements: Horizontal (zero) slopes at each point where $x = 0$
alternatives.correct_example: At every lattice point on the $y$-axis, tangent segment is horizontal.

common_traps: Drawing vertical segments (infinite slope) at $x = 0$ — but numerator is $-2x$, which is $0$ there
scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point of 2 in part (a) is earned for correct zero slopes at indicated points where $x = 0$.

sample_responses.response: Horizontal tick marks at all lattice points on the $y$-axis.
sample_responses.commentary: Slope $= -2x/y = 0$ whenever $x = 0$ (and $y \ne 0$), so segments are horizontal.

sample_responses.response: Vertical tick marks at all lattice points on the $y$-axis.
sample_responses.commentary: Vertical segments correspond to undefined slope (division by zero), which happens on the $x$-axis, not the $y$-axis.

scoring_points.description: Correct nonzero slopes (sign correct in each quadrant)

required_elements: Slopes are negative in Q1 (where $x>0, y>0$)
Slopes are positive in Q2 (where $x<0, y>0$)
Slopes are positive in Q3 (where $x<0, y<0$)
Slopes are negative in Q4 (where $x>0, y<0$)

common_traps: Using the unsigned formula $2x/y$ instead of $-2x/y$
scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point of 2 in part (a) is earned for correct nonzero slopes with correct sign in all relevant quadrants.

sample_responses.response: Segments tilt down-right in Q1 and Q3; tilt up-right in Q2 and Q4.
sample_responses.commentary: Sign of slope $-2x/y$ matches each quadrant correctly.
Ignores the sign change of $-2x/y$ across quadrants.

parts.prompt: Let $y = f(x)$ be the particular solution to the differential equation with the initial condition $f(1) = -1$. Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $f$ at $(1, -1)$ and use it to approximate $f(1.1)$.

scoring_points.description: Correct equation of tangent line at $(1, -1)$
common_traps: Using $-2$ instead of $2$ for the slope (sign error from $-2(1)/(-1)$)
scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point of 2 in part (b) is earned for the correct tangent-line equation $y + 1 = 2(x - 1)$.

sample_responses.commentary: Correct slope evaluation and correct point-slope form.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point of 2 in part (b) is earned for the linear approximation $f(1.1) \approx -0.8$.

sample_responses.commentary: Uses $\Delta x = 0.1$ correctly in the local linearization.
Plugged $x = 1.1$ directly rather than the displacement $\Delta x = 0.1$.

parts.prompt: Find the particular solution $y = f(x)$ to the given differential equation with the initial condition $f(1) = -1$.

common_traps: Not separating variables at all — scoring note says 0/5 if no separation of variables
scoring_points.official_rubric: The first point of 5 in part (c) is earned for separating variables correctly: $y\, dy = -2x\, dx$. Note: no separation of variables gives 0 out of 5.

sample_responses.commentary: Multiplies both sides by $y\, dx$ to separate correctly.
Did not separate; integrating right side directly with $y$ still in it cannot proceed.

scoring_points.official_rubric: The second point of 5 in part (c) is earned for correct antiderivatives $\tfrac{y^2}{2}$ and $-x^2$.

common_traps: Missing $+C$: max $2/5$ points if no constant of integration
scoring_points.official_rubric: The third point of 5 in part (c) is earned for including the constant of integration. Scoring note: max 2 out of 5 if no constant of integration appears.

sample_responses.commentary: Missing $+C$; caps score at 2/5 for this part.

common_traps: Substituting into the derivative instead of the integrated equation
scoring_points.official_rubric: The fourth point of 5 in part (c) is earned for correctly using the initial condition to find $C = \tfrac{3}{2}$.

sample_responses.commentary: Correctly plugs $(1, -1)$ into the antiderivative equation.
Arithmetic error in solving for $C$; correct value is $\tfrac{3}{2}$.

scoring_points.description: Solves for $y$ and selects negative branch
common_traps: Taking the positive branch $y = +\sqrt{3 - 2x^2}$ — inconsistent with $y(1) = -1$
scoring_points.official_rubric: The fifth point of 5 in part (c) is earned for solving for $y$ as $y = -\sqrt{3 - 2x^2}$, choosing the negative branch consistent with the initial condition.

sample_responses.commentary: Selects the negative branch correctly based on the initial condition.
At $x = 1$ this gives $y = 1$, contradicting $f(1) = -1$. Must take the negative branch.
